column1: C[joeplaysgo: black tries to put pressure on the lower group and the single N16 stone with this move
C[joeplaysgo: this move by white changed black's outlook very quickly
C[joeplaysgo: would M15 have been better here?
karaklis: M15 would have caught a fight there to settle things. Black would have gained the upper side and white would have enlarged the moyo on the left.
joeplaysgo: you are right... that's probably why i went with K14... (but white still got a good moyo!)
C[joeplaysgo: black is helping white build a strong moyo!
karaklis: Yes, white was happy to give up a few stones to create a strong moyo.
joeplaysgo: I'm going to remember this lesson!  :)

C[joeplaysgo: blacks capture of 2 stones and <10 points of territory are nothing in comparison to the large wall at g13 that now gives white all the territory at the bottom... black has lost and now tries to trick white into making mistakes
C[karaklis: Maybe this would have been the right time for black to invade at c3. This is sente for black, so after the corner joseki black could still have set at m15
joeplaysgo: yes, thank you ... very good point...
C[karaklis: I think this would have been the time to invade c3. That is much bigger than the few points black would have lost around L15
C[karaklis: This seems to be a thankyou move, because it helps white to make good shape at m12. Black's alternative would have beeen to set m12 himself.
C[joeplaysgo: black had planned to play L13 but made a last minute mistake...
C[joeplaysgo: black know the match is over, but tries a last ditch effort
C[karaklis: This allows white to separate the black groups

joeplaysgo: true... black was really making a mess here and trying to get an opportunity to form an attack in the G11 area...  I considered just resigning before trying this invasion.  I knew you would have very solid play.  (But other players have told me that I resigned too soon in my past games...)
C[joeplaysgo: good solid play by white doesn't allow black to live in any area...
C[joeplaysgo: Great game!  Thanks for playing!  I learned a lot...
karaklis: Thank you for the game and the review.
joeplaysgo: Your comments were well received and I will take them to heart in future matches.  Thank you!

DanielBR: I disagree. Because of D4.
TedSarvata: D14, D14 and D4 all are pointed at influence - C14 is more balanced.

DanielBR: Q4 is fine. Instead of K3 I prefer j3, because it prevents W-H3. C10 is urgent, because it protects the weakness of D14.
DanielBR: No! If B-18, then W-E10 is a good offense. Also, I hope a gote related to D18.

C[karaklis: Here a few comments about the game.
This move was too passive. d7 makes more territory
C[karaklis: try to find out what you wanted to achieve with this move. better move here: e2. this threatens to enter white's corner after white blocks with f2, black can go to e7

C[karaklis: This is the so called ""empty triangle"" (formed by the triangle stones e4, e5, d5, and empty because d4 is empty. This is a very inefficient shape. Remember this and avoid it in your next games.
C[karaklis: have you read out the situation whether g3 captures something?
C[karaklis: h4 would be more troublesome for white here

C[karaklis: Before this move the g5-stone had one liberty (at g6). After this move the g5-g6 group still had only one liberty. And they cannot escape, even if white plays elsewhere. Try to read it out. The tipp here: count the liberties of your groups.
C[karaklis: the g2 group cannot live, it can only make one eye (h1 and j2 are miai for white to kill this group). It would be better to block at c2 or c8
C[karaklis: f8 cannot live, since white can capture the g6 group at any time. better to block at c8

C[karaklis: this move doesnt help the black bottom corner group to survive. instead the big black d5 group is in danger, since it must make two eyes to live. b6 is the right move here. after that black could make a second eye at b5.
end of comments. hope this helps and good luck for your next games.
karaklis: Have fun and good luck
